obverse.description:
Dark blue print on predominantly light blue multicoloured underprint; serial number black.
Blue on multicolor overprint.
Benito Juárez at right, scale and book to left. On left edge, eagle printed in variable color ink and quote from the Manifesto to the Nation in 1887.
Issuer name on top to left, face value in numbers on top right corner, in numbers and letters on lower left, in letters on top, below issuer name.
Location and issuing date, series on right edge, rotated 90º. Black serial #s 7 digits with 1 letter prefix on lower left. 2 signatures with titles below, above serial #s.
Perfect match printing device with Mexican map shape, that can be complete when looked with backlight. Transparent window with raised number "20" and linear design on lower left.
Printer name on right bottom.
obverse.lettering:
Banco de México
Veinte Pesos 20
MÉXICO D. F. , 24 JUN. 2011
SERIE P
QUE EL PUEBLO Y EL GOBIERNO
RESPETEN LOS DERECHOS DE TODOS.
ENTRE LOS INDIVIDUOS, COMO ENTRE LAS 
NACIONES. EL RESPETO AL DERECHO AJENO
ES LA PAZ.
MANIFIESTO A LA NACIÓN, 15 DE JULIO DE 1867.
20
PESOS
JUNTA DE GOBIERNO CAJERO PRINCIPAL
M4552180
BENITO JUÁREZ
BANCO DE MÉXICO
obverse.lettering_translation:
Bank of México
Twenty Pesos
México, Federal District, June 24th., 2011
That the people and the government respect the rights of everyone. Between individuals, as between nations, respect for the rights of others, it is the peace
Manifesto to the Nation, July 15th., 1867
20 Pesos
Government Board, Main Cashier

reverse.description:
Dark blue print on multicolor underprint.
City of Monte Albán, Oaxaca, Mexico.
Seal of the Bank of Mexico top left of image.
Mask of Cocijo at lower right of image. Face value in numbers on top left, in numbers and letters on lower right, in letters below image, to left.
Issuer name on top,
reverse.lettering:
Banco de México
MONTE ALBÁN, OAX.
20 
Pesos
COCIJO
Veinte Pesos
reverse.lettering_translation:
Bank of México
Monte Albán, Oaxaca
20 Pesos, Twenty Pesos
